meta.notes: Augmented ancestry/general/skill feats from local Foundry checkout (max 2000).

feats.name: Aberration Kinship
feats.description: Your mind resonates with the inscrutable thought processes that inhuman aberrations use to communicate. You can cast @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Mindlink] as an innate occult spell once per day, but you can target only aberrations.

feats.name: Adapted Cantrip
feats.description: Through study of multiple magical traditions, you've altered a spell to suit your spellcasting style. Choose one cantrip from a magical tradition other than your own. You can cast that cantrip as a spell of your class's tradition.

feats.name: Adaptive Vision
feats.description: Access You are from Dongun Hold or Alkenstar.

feats.name: Adhyabhau
feats.description: You descend from the rare psychic vampires known as vetalaranas.

feats.name: Adroit Manipulation
feats.description: Access Mihrini ethnicity You've fiddled with knots, locks, and blacksmith's puzzles since you were a child to give your hands something to do.

feats.name: Aegis of the Dissolution
feats.description: You are a friend of shadows and know how to avoid the troubles they bring. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against effects with the darkness or shadow traits.

feats.name: Aeonbound
feats.description: Your lineage is influenced or directly tied to the supernatural inhabitants of the Perfect City of Axis, a realm of pure and absolute order.

feats.name: Ageless Spirit
feats.description: You remember more about your previous incarnations than most leshys.

feats.name: Air Cushion
feats.description: Pockets of air slow your fall. You take no damage from falls of 25 feet or less and treat all falls as if they were 25 feet shorter.

feats.name: Aiuvarin Lore
feats.description: You carry the ideals and background knowledge of your elven parentage. You gain the trained proficiency rank in Diplomacy and Society. If you would automatically become trained in one of those skills, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You also become trained in Elven Lore.

feats.name: Alabaster Eyes
feats.description: In place of the normal vishkanyan golden eyes, you were born with especially perceptive snow-white eyes. You gain darkvision. Special You can take this feat only at 1st level, and you can't retrain out of this feat or into this feat.

feats.name: Alchemical Scholar
feats.description: You gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feats-srd.Item.Alchemical Crafting] feat.

feats.name: Alghollthu Bound
feats.description: Although you may not even be aware, the alghollthus your ancestors once served maintain a stranglehold on the deepest, tethered parts of your mind.

feats.name: All This Will Happen Again
feats.description: Trigger You fail or critically fail a Will save against an emotion effect.

feats.name: Ambersoul
feats.description: Your elemental heritage is reflected in the oils and fragrances of plants, in tree resin that fossilizes into amber, or in the gentle smell of a flower.

feats.name: Anadi Lore
feats.description: You've learned how to provide for your community, be it through hard-earned sustenance or useful crafts.

feats.name: Ancestral Insight
feats.description: You have extensive knowledge of your ancestors' ancient origins.

feats.name: Ancestral Linguistics
feats.description: Over your extensive lifespan, you've studied many languages.

feats.name: Ancestral Longevity
feats.description: You have accumulated a vast array of lived knowledge over the years. During your daily preparations, you can become trained in one skill of your choice. This proficiency lasts until the next time you prepare. Since this proficiency is temporary, you can't use it as a prerequisite for a skill increase or a permanent character option.

feats.name: Ancient Memories
feats.description: In the long years since Ghorus's day, you've done pretty much everything at one time or another.

feats.name: Android Lore
feats.description: You have a keen interest in the origins of your people.

feats.name: Angelkin
feats.description: You descend from an angel, a winged messenger from Nirvana or one of the other celestial realms.

feats.name: Animal Accomplice
feats.description: You build a rapport with an animal, which becomes magically bonded to you. You gain a familiar. The type of animal is up to you, but most gnomes choose animals with a burrow Speed.

feats.name: Animal Elocutionist
feats.description: You hear animal sounds as conversations instead of unintelligent noise, and you can respond in turn.

feats.name: Animal Senses
feats.description: Your beast heritage grants you keen senses. You gain either low-light vision or scent (imprecise) 30 feet.

feats.name: Aphorite Lore
feats.description: Growing up with a connection to the Plane of Law grants keen understanding of order. You become trained in Deception and Society. If you would automatically become trained in one of those skills, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You also become trained in Axis Lore.

feats.name: Aquatic Eyes
feats.description: As your connection to water grows, you can see more easily in places with less light, such as the depths of the ocean.

feats.name: Aqueous Dragonblood
feats.description: Your blood surges with the might of dragons who master the waters, and you can move freely in their domain.

feats.name: Arcane Communication
feats.description: You can deliver silent messages. You gain touch telepathy, allowing you to communicate silently and mentally with any creature you're touching, as long as you share a language. Enhancement You can send your messages even further. Your telepathy gains a range …

feats.name: Arcane Dragonblood
feats.description: You descend from a dragon that wields mastery of their magical abilities, such as a fortune dragon or mirage dragon.

feats.name: Arcane Eye
feats.description: Your eye has been magically enhanced to pierce darkness.

feats.name: Arcane Tattoos
feats.description: Access Varisian ethnicity or New Thassilon nationality You have tattoos on your body corresponding to one of the ancient Thassilonian schools of magic.

feats.name: Ardande Lore
feats.description: Your wood elemental heritage grants you innate understanding of the natural world. You become trained in Nature and Survival. If you would automatically become trained in one of those skills, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You also become trained in Plant Lore.

feats.name: Artisanal Crafter
feats.description: Like many minotaurs, you take pride in creating items made from stone or metal.

feats.name: As in Life, So in Death
feats.description: Specific memories of your old life are hard to hold onto, but you know things without remembering why.

feats.name: Ash-Piercing Gaze
feats.description: Neither cinders nor winds shall prevent you from seeing those who need help. You automatically succeed at the flat check to target a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Concealed] creature if that creature is concealed only by smoke or mist.

feats.name: Ask the Bones
feats.description: Frequency once per day You keep the bone of an ancestor or a friend to call upon for advice. Attempt to Recall Knowledge. If the bones belonged to a person particularly knowledgeable on the subject, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your check.

feats.name: Athamaru Lore
feats.description: You've immersed yourself in community learning, particularly around navigating the waters and tending to animals.

feats.name: Athamaru Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You wield weapons designed to work underwater with ease.

feats.name: Automaton Armament
feats.description: You've been provided a body part designed for combat.

feats.name: Automaton Lore
feats.description: You have come to better understand the process that made your body and the magic that powers it.

feats.name: Avenge in Glory
feats.description: Frequency once per day Access mountain dwarf ethnicity Trigger An ally within 30 feet gains the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Dying] condition.

feats.name: Avowed Insight
feats.description: You become trained in the Lore skill corresponding to one creature type mentioned by your vow (for example, Fey Lore if your heritage is deny the firstborn pursuit).
prerequisites: Yaksha heritage with an edict that requires you to confront a certain type of creature

feats.name: Awakened Animal Lore
feats.description: You have taken the time to learn about the process of awakening and the experiences of your fellow awakened animals.

feats.name: Awakened Jewel
feats.description: You once held the head gem of a deceased ancestor or loved one, and it awakened a mysterious power in your own.

feats.name: Awakened Magic
feats.description: When you awakened, primal magic was released within you. Choose one cantrip from the primal spell list. You can cast this spell as an primal innate spell at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell rank equal to half your level rounded up.

feats.name: Axiomatic Lore
feats.description: You possess a strong understanding of process among mortals and lawful outsiders alike.

feats.name: Azarketi Lore
feats.description: You have learned the history and origins of your people and how to connect to both your land and sea heritage.

feats.name: Azarketi Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You are familiar with weapons that excel underwater.

feats.name: Bamboo and Silt Repose
feats.description: At home among mire and copse, you flit from river to tree with a dragonfly's shimmering grace.

feats.name: Bark Skin
feats.description: Your skin has a rough, bark-like texture. You gain resistance to slashing damage equal to half your level (minimum 1).

feats.name: Battleblooded
feats.description: You descend from those whose lives were touched by legendary einherjars, who were crafted from the souls of mighty warriors slain in battle and chosen by valkyries to serve as foot soldiers to many gods.

feats.name: Beast Trainer
feats.description: You have an impressive innate ability to tame and command ferocious beasts.

feats.name: Beastbrood
feats.description: One of your ancestors was a rakshasa, the corrupt and aristocratic reincarnated souls that call Golarion home.

feats.name: Beastkin Lore
feats.description: You know the ways of the wild from your animal heritage. You become trained in Nature and Survival. If you would automatically become trained in one of those skills, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You also become trained in a Lore skill related to your animal type.

feats.name: Benefactor's Resistance
feats.description: Your scales have taken on the characteristics of your draconic benefactor and insulate you from certain damage types.

feats.name: Bestial Manifestation
feats.description: Part of your body has an animalistic influence from a planar creature.

feats.name: Bone Magic
feats.description: Your connection to your iruxi ancestors manifests as a simple primal spell that you cast using a fragment of an ancestor's bones.

feats.name: Bouncy Goblin
feats.description: You have a particular elasticity that makes it easy for you to bounce and squish.

feats.name: Breath of the Dragon (Dragonblood)
feats.description: Tapping into the physiology of your draconic ancestor, you can exhale a torrent of energy in a @Template[cone|distance:15] or a @Template[line|distance:30], dealing 1d4 damage.

feats.name: Brightsoul
feats.description: The fire inside you asserts itself as beaming, radiant light.

feats.name: Brine May
feats.description: Your mother was a sea hag, granting you one sea-green or blue eye.

feats.name: Brinesoul
feats.description: Your elemental ancestor's legacy asserts itself as brackish water, like the seawater of the ocean. You gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Salt Wound] reaction.

feats.name: Burn It!
feats.description: Fire fascinates you. Your spells and alchemical items that deal fire damage gain a status bonus to damage equal to half the spell's rank or one-quarter the item's level (minimum bonus +1). You also gain a +1 status bonus to any persistent fire damage you deal.

feats.name: Callow May
feats.description: You are among the most common of changelings—those born to sweet hags—and a vibrant green eye signifies that parentage.

feats.name: Canopy Sight
feats.description: You can see equally well even in dense jungles where only a small amount of light shines through. You gain low-light vision.

feats.name: Cantorian Reinforcement
feats.description: The life energy that helped create the first hobgoblins is particularly potent in you, protecting you from ongoing maladies.

feats.name: Caretaker's Intuition
feats.description: Your forebears had a deep understanding of the natural world around them, and you've learned some of their knowledge and techniques.

feats.name: Caretaker's Restoration
feats.description: Frequency once per day Trigger An item you're holding, wearing, or wielding becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Broken] but not destroyed.

feats.name: Cat Nap
feats.description: Frequency once per hour You can regain your energy quickly with a brief nap. You sleep for 10 minutes, after which you gain temporary Hit Points equal to your level that last for 1 hour. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feat-effects.Item.Effect: Cat Nap]

feats.name: Cat's Luck
feats.description: Frequency once per day Trigger You fail a Reflex saving throw. You instinctively twist away from danger. You can reroll the triggering saving throw and use the better result.

feats.name: Catch the Details
feats.description: You are quick at recognizing broad details about people and things around you.

feats.name: Catfolk Dance
feats.description: You have a habit of always being in the way when other creatures attempt to move.

feats.name: Catfolk Lore
feats.description: Growing up among catfolk has taught you the traditional values of freedom, stewardship, and quick reactions to trouble.

feats.name: Catfolk Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You gain access to all uncommon weapons with the catfolk trait.

feats.name: Cattle Speech
feats.description: You can understand the lowing of cattle and similar animals as its own language.

feats.name: Caustic Nectar
feats.description: You have specialized glands inside your throat, which produce acidic nectar that you can spit long distances.
prerequisites: ardande trait, plant trait, or wood trait

feats.name: Cel Rau
feats.description: You are a cel rau, child of a strigoi, one of the first vampires to walk Golarion.

feats.name: Celestial Resistance
feats.description: Your planar heritage grants you resilience. Choose one energy type related to your lineage (fire for hellspawn, cold for musetouched, etc.). You gain resistance to that damage type equal to half your level (minimum 1).

feats.name: Centaur Lore
feats.description: You've learned how to nurture your environment and to care for your people.

feats.name: Centaur Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You've trained in the weapons favored by your people, becoming a skilled archer and jouster.

feats.name: Ceremony of Protection
feats.description: You manipulate your exoskeleton to overlap itself. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC until the start of your next turn. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feat-effects.Item.Effect: Ceremony of Protection]

feats.name: Ceremony of the Evened Hand
feats.description: You have cultivated your exoskeleton's form for close combat, using knots, whorls, and reinforced branches.

feats.name: Chance Death
feats.description: Frequency once per day Trigger You would die as a result of a failed recovery check or saving throw.

feats.name: Changeling Lore
feats.description: Your hag mother left you knowledge of dark magic. You become trained in Deception and Occultism. If you would automatically become trained in one of those skills, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You also become trained in Hag Lore.

feats.name: Chaotic Resistance
feats.description: The chaos in your blood makes you unpredictable. At the start of each day, roll 1d4 to determine your resistance: 1 = acid, 2 = electricity, 3 = sonic, 4 = no resistance. You gain resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1) to the rolled type.

feats.name: Charmed Sleep
feats.description: When you sleep, you turn into an inanimate object a little smaller than a human's thumb—easily overlooked and carried.

feats.name: Cheek Pouches
feats.description: Your cheeks are stretchy, and you can store up to four items of light Bulk or less in these cheek pouches.

feats.name: Childlike Plant
feats.description: You're adept at hiding your plantlike features to pass as a human.
prerequisites: chrysanthemum leshy heritage or peachchild leshy heritage

feats.name: Cindersoul
feats.description: The fire of your elemental ancestor manifests like dying embers of a blaze, and your inner charcoal helps staunch bleeding, purify simple poisons, and absorb acid.

feats.name: City Scavenger
feats.description: You know that the greatest treasures often look like refuse.

feats.name: Clan Lore
feats.description: Your identity as a member of your clan is as central to your sense of self as your dwarven ancestry.

feats.name: Clan Pistol
feats.description: Access You are from Dongun Hold or Alkenstar.

feats.name: Clan's Edge
feats.description: By moving your clan dagger with rapid precision, you can protect yourself more effectively.

feats.name: Cleansing Subroutine
feats.description: Your nanites help purge your body of harmful chemicals and toxins.

feats.name: Climbing Tail
feats.description: Your tail makes it much easier for you to climb.

feats.name: Clone-Risen
feats.description: You were formed from a clone ritual or alchemical experiment. Created as a vessel for another creature, you can suppress your sentience momentarily, making your mind harder to alter. You gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Empty Vessel] reaction.

feats.name: Cold Minded
feats.description: The subtle strands of beguiling magic leave little impression on your mind.

feats.name: Collapse
feats.description: Frequency once per 10 minutes Trigger An enemy's Strike against you is a critical hit.

feats.name: Community Knowledge
feats.description: Frequency once per hour Trigger You attempt a skill check requiring 3 actions or fewer. You commune with the psychic echoes of your ancestors and community, channeling their experiences into yourself. You gain a +2 status bonus on the triggering skill check.

feats.name: Community-Minded
feats.description: You are familiar with all the members of your community and their typical movements, and you have learned how to apply these patterns outside those circles.

feats.name: Conrasu Lore
feats.description: You have learned about your history and your people from shapers.

feats.name: Conrasu Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You have taken it upon yourself to learn the ways of combat to aid with your tasks, perhaps in a role as a corrector.

feats.name: Consult the Stars
feats.description: You find wisdom in the movements of the heavens.

feats.name: Cooperative Nature
feats.description: The short human life span lends perspective and has taught you from a young age to set aside differences and work with others to achieve greatness. You gain a +4 circumstance bonus on checks to Aid.

feats.name: Coral Symbiotes
feats.description: You've tended to your coral jewelry so well that you have formed a symbiotic relationship with it.

feats.name: Corgi Mount
feats.description: The smallest of fey have ridden corgis as mounts since time immemorial, leading to a pattern on corgis' backs called a "faerie saddle." You have formed a magical connection with a corgi that can serve as your mount.

feats.name: Courteous Comeback
feats.description: Access Keleshite nationality Requirements You're in a settlement or community, and you haven't used Courteous Comeback in this settlement or community within the past month.

feats.name: Covet Hoard
feats.description: You have a dragon's desire for treasure. You gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feats-srd.Item.Hefty Hauler] skill feat, even if you don't meet the prerequisites. You also gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your Perception DC against attempts to [[/act steal]] from …

feats.name: Crawling Form
feats.description: You can transform into a small animal or celestial creature to scurry and spy.
prerequisites: born of animal or born of celestial heritage

feats.name: Creative Prodigy
feats.description: You have a powerful creative drive that is clear to any who interact with you.

feats.name: Cringe
feats.description: Trigger A creature you are aware of critically succeeds on a Strike against you and would deal damage to you.

feats.name: Critter Shape
feats.description: Once per hour, you can use @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Change Shape] to enter a critter shape.

feats.name: Croak Talker
feats.description: You can communicate with the simplest of frogs and other similar creatures using long-winded croaks. You can ask questions of, receive answers from, and use the Diplomacy skill with animals that are amphibians (most amphibians have the amphibious trait).

feats.name: Crocodile's Twin
feats.description: Your egg contained your twin, a crocodile. Considered a rare and auspicious event, you share a magical bond with this sibling. You gain a tiny crocodile as a familiar. Special You can take this feat only at 1st level.
prerequisites: bakuwa lizardfolk heritage or makari lizardfolk heritage

feats.name: Crown of Bone
feats.description: When you came of age, you were blessed by the Mother Earth with a thickened skull, which was celebrated as an auspicious sign among your community.

feats.name: Crunch
feats.description: Your jaws can crush bone and bite through armor. Your jaws unarmed attack deals 1d8 piercing damage instead of 1d6 and gains the grapple trait

feats.name: Crystal Luminescence
feats.description: Your horn glows with bioluminescent color, casting bright light in a @Template[emanation|distance:20] (and dim light for the next 20 feet).

feats.name: Cynical
feats.description: You've been approached with suspicion and distrust throughout your life and return these sentiments back to the strangers you encounter.

feats.name: Dance of the Mousedeer
feats.description: You move your body in a pattern that evokes the small and clever Sister Mousedeer, misdirecting enemies and filling their vision with shadows to hide behind.

feats.name: Deep Vision
feats.description: Your vision is suited for night or the underground. You gain darkvision.

feats.name: Deliberate Death
feats.description: Frequency once per day Trigger You are about to gain the dying condition as a result of another creature's attack or ability, and that creature is within your melee reach.

feats.name: Demonbane Warrior
feats.description: You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to damage with weapons and unarmed attacks against demons.

feats.name: Devil's Advocate
feats.description: Access Chelaxian nationality You know more about the habits of devils than is entirely safe.

feats.name: Dhampir Lore
feats.description: Your undead parentage has taught you the ways of darkness. You become trained in Deception and Medicine. If you would automatically become trained in one of those skills, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You also become trained in Vampire Lore.

feats.name: Dig Quickly
feats.description: Requirements You are standing on dirt, sand, snow, or similar particulate material.

feats.name: Distracting Shadows
feats.description: You have learned to remain hidden by using larger folk as a distraction to avoid drawing attention to yourself.

feats.name: Divine Dragonblood
feats.description: You can trace your lineage to a dragon with almost deific powers, such as a diabolic dragon or empyreal dragon.

feats.name: Dokkaebi Fire
feats.description: You can create illusory wisps of ghostly blue flame.

feats.name: Dongun Education
feats.description: Access You are from Dongun Hold or Alkenstar.

feats.name: Draconic Aspect
feats.description: You have an obvious draconic feature, such as sharp claws, a snout full of sharp teeth, or strong reptilian tail, that you can use offensively.

feats.name: Draconic Resistance
feats.description: Your dragon blood grants you resistance. Choose acid, cold, electricity, fire, or poison (matching your draconic lineage). You gain resistance to that damage type equal to half your level (minimum 1).

feats.name: Draconic Sight
feats.description: You have a fraction of the senses of your draconic forebear. You gain low-light vision, or you gain darkvision if your ancestry already grants you low-light vision.

feats.name: Draconic Sycophant
feats.description: You possess features that dragons find particularly cute or charming, making it easier to endear yourself to them, or at least cause them to momentarily forgive your insolence.

feats.name: Dragon Lore
feats.description: You have an innate understanding of dragon-kind. You become trained in Arcana and Intimidation. If you would automatically become trained in one of those skills, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You also become trained in Dragon Lore.

feats.name: Dragon Spit
feats.description: Many Tian-Dan claim to have dragon blood in their veins, and in your case, this is true-you can spit energy, and you might have an especially visible sign of your draconic heritage.

feats.name: Dragon's Presence
feats.description: As kin to dragonkind, you project unflappable confidence (that collapses catastrophically against the deadliest foes).

feats.name: Dragonet Breath
feats.description: You have developed your magical breath. You expel the dragonet breath associated with your heritage. Dragonet Breath gains the traits listed in parentheses, and the DC is your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. After you use Dragonet Breath, you can't…

feats.name: Dragonet Resistances
feats.description: You might be a little dragon, but you are still a dragon, which means it's difficult to constrain you. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saves against sleep effects and effects that would make you @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Paralyzed].

feats.name: Dragonscaled Lore
feats.description: The study of dragons is not merely academic to you but part of your family tree.

feats.name: Dream May
feats.description: You are the child of a cuckoo hag, making one of your eyes violet or even black.

feats.name: Dromaar Lore
feats.description: Your orc heritage makes you tough and fierce. You become trained in Athletics and Intimidation. If you would automatically become trained in one of those skills, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You also become trained in Orc Lore.

feats.name: Dualborn
feats.description: Your suli heritage can be traced back to two geniekin ancestors of opposing elements, or to an elemental ancestor that embodied two combined elements.

feats.name: Duskwalker Lore
feats.description: The Boneyard has imparted knowledge of the cycle of souls. You become trained in Religion and Nature. If you would automatically become trained in one of those skills, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You also become trained in Boneyard Lore.

feats.name: Duskwalker Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You have familiarity with the bo staff, longbow, composite longbow, scythe, and staff—for the purpose of proficiency, you treat any of these that are martial weapons as simple weapons and any that are advanced weapons as martial weapons.

feats.name: Dustsoul
feats.description: Your earth elemental ancestor's influence manifests in you as dust and dirt, like the soil of a field or the hot desert sands.

feats.name: Dwarven Doughtiness
feats.description: You are naturally calm and collected in the face of imminent danger. At the end of your turn, reduce your @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened] condition by 2 instead of 1.

feats.name: Dwarven Lore
feats.description: You eagerly absorbed the old stories and traditions of your ancestors, your gods, and your people, studying subjects and techniques passed down for generation upon generation.

feats.name: Dwarven Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: Your kin have instilled in you an affinity for hard-hitting weapons, and you prefer these to more elegant arms.

feats.name: Earned Glory
feats.description: Elves often struggle with underestimating aiuvarins, and you are experienced at telling stories of your accomplishments to gain their respect.

feats.name: Earth Resistance
feats.description: Your earth elemental blood grants you physical resilience. You gain resistance to bludgeoning damage equal to half your level (minimum 1).

feats.name: Eidetic Ear
feats.description: You have an amazing memory for sound. You gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.feats-srd.Item.Assurance]{Assurance (Performance)} feat. With a successful @Check[flat|dc:8], you can accurately recall a sound or a snippet of conversation you heard within the last wee…

feats.name: Elemental Assault
feats.description: You can shroud your weapons in elemental energy. Choose acid, cold, electricity, or fire. Once per day, for 1 minute, your Strikes deal an additional 1d6 damage of the chosen type.

feats.name: Elemental Embellish
feats.description: You can summon a harmless but impressive elemental display.

feats.name: Elemental Eyes
feats.description: You can see in the darkness as easily as an elemental. You gain darkvision. Special You can select this feat only at 1st level, and you can't retrain into or out of this feat.

feats.name: Elemental Lore
feats.description: You've devoted yourself to researching the secrets of the Inner Sphere.

feats.name: Elemental Trade
feats.description: You have a strong connection with stone and metal.

feats.name: Elf Atavism
feats.description: Your elven blood runs particularly strong, granting you features far more elven than those of a typical aiuvarin.

feats.name: Elucidating Vision
feats.description: Your eyes have seen many things in your past lives, and your sight seems to have grown more powerful over your lifetimes. You gain darkvision.

feats.name: Elven Aloofness
feats.description: As much as you might care for them, you've come to terms with the ephemeral nature of non-elves, and it makes their threats feel less troublesome.

feats.name: Elven Instincts
feats.description: Your elven blood grants you sharper senses. You gain low-light vision. If you already have low-light vision, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks made in dim light.

feats.name: Elven Lore
feats.description: You've studied traditional elven arts, learning about arcane magic and the world around you. You become trained in Arcana and Nature. If you would automatically become trained in one of those skills (from your background or class, for example), you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You also become trained in Elven Lore.

feats.name: Elven Verve
feats.description: While all elves are immune to the paralyzing touch of ghouls, you can shake off flesh-numbing magic of all kinds.

feats.name: Elven Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You favor bows and other elegant weapons. You are trained with longbows, composite longbows, longswords, rapiers, shortbows, and composite shortbows. In addition, you gain access to all uncommon elf weapons. For the purpose of determining your proficiency, martial elf weapons are simple weapons and advanced elf weapons are martial weapons.

feats.name: Elver Pet
feats.description: Athamarus have bonded with eels more closely than any other fish.

feats.name: Ember's Eyes
feats.description: Your eyes blaze with inner flame. You gain low-light vision, or you gain darkvision if your ancestry already has low-light vision. Special If your ancestry has neither low-light vision nor darkvision, you can take this ancestry feat a second time to gain dark…

feats.name: Emberkin
feats.description: A peri's roaring spirit burns in your soul.

feats.name: Embodied Legionary Subjectivity
feats.description: Access You're from Nex. Nex's Fleshforges shaped you not to achieve individual greatness at arms, but rather to become a part of a legion, a modular piece integral to the combined arms stratagems of Nexian battlemages. You become trained in Arcana and Athleti…

feats.name: Emit Defensive Odor
feats.description: Frequency once per day While athamarus' natural pheromones are typically used for communication, you have developed yours into a defense mechanism to ward off foes.

feats.name: Emotional Partitions
feats.description: You have created strong mental partitions in your mind to filter out negative emotional influences.

feats.name: Emotionless
feats.description: Your malfunctioning emotional processors make it difficult for you to feel strong emotions.

feats.name: Empathetic Plea
feats.description: Trigger You are attacked by a creature that you haven't yet acted hostile toward.

feats.name: Energy Beam
feats.description: You can channel your core's power through the magical gem that serves as your eye.

feats.name: Esteemed Visitor
feats.description: Your polite manners and natural ability to comfort others make it easy for you to break down cultural barriers and earn strangers' trust.

feats.name: Evanescent Wings
feats.description: You've manifested wings that can flutter for brief spurts. You Fly. If you don't normally have a fly Speed, you gain a fly Speed of 15 feet for this movement. If you aren't on solid ground at the end of this movement, you fall.

feats.name: Everyday Form
feats.description: There's nothing better than being a tanuki, but sometimes you need a less conspicuous form when going into the big city or other crowded places.

feats.name: Explosive Savant
feats.description: Access You are from Dongun Hold or Alkenstar.

feats.name: Extra Squishy
feats.description: Your rubbery physique makes it easier for you to wedge yourself into tight spaces and more difficult for your enemies to dislodge you.

feats.name: Eye for Masonry
feats.description: Your familiarity with architecture allows you to easily spot subtle stonework.

feats.name: Eye for Treasure
feats.description: You know good artisanship when you see it and can wax poetic about crafting techniques and forms.

feats.name: Eyes of Night
feats.description: You can see in the darkness as easily as a true vampire. You gain darkvision. Special You can select this feat only at 1st level, and you can't retrain into or out of this feat.

feats.name: Fang Sharpener
feats.description: You have filed your teeth into jagged points and have an unusually powerful jaw, making your mouth a dangerous weapon.
prerequisites: irongut goblin or razortooth goblin heritage

feats.name: Fangs
feats.description: Your vampiric heritage has given you sharp fangs. You gain a fangs unarmed attack that deals 1d6 piercing damage. Your fangs are in the brawling group.

feats.name: Fascinated by Society
feats.description: Just as some humanoids find themselves driven to study nature, you are obsessed with the artificial constructs of society and can't get enough.

feats.name: Faultspawn
feats.description: You bear a grudge, inherited from a blasphemous asura forebear in your bloodline, against a particular deity and their works. You gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Find Fault] reaction.

feats.name: Ferrousoul
feats.description: Whether in their pure elemental forms or as part of an alloy such as steel, the elemental metal in your soul resonates strongly with iron, bronze, and other sturdy, practical metals suitable for weaponry.

feats.name: Fetchling Lore
feats.description: You've learned obscure lessons about your fellow fetchlings.

feats.name: Fey Cantrips
feats.description: You've learned the cantrips sprites have used since time immemorial.

feats.name: Fey Fellowship
feats.description: Your enhanced fey connection (either through your blood or via prolonged contact with their kind) affords you a warmer reception from creatures of the First World as well as tools to foil their tricks or withstand their magic.

feats.name: Fire Resistance
feats.description: Your fire elemental blood grants resistance to fire equal to half your level (minimum 1).

feats.name: Fire Savvy
feats.description: Access You are from Dongun Hold or Alkenstar.

feats.name: First World Magic
feats.description: Your connection to the First World grants you a primal innate spell, much like those of the fey.

feats.name: Fisheye
feats.description: Frequency once per day You can gain the magic of the seas by eating your favorite seafood.

feats.name: Fledgling Flight
feats.description: Frequency once per round You can fly through the air in short bursts. You Fly. If you don't normally have a fly Speed, you gain a fly Speed of 15 feet for this movement. If you aren't on solid ground at the end of this movement, you fall.

feats.name: Flexible Form
feats.description: Your body is as supple as a sapling, capable of bending without breaking.

feats.name: Folk Healer
feats.description: Your community recognizes you as a shaman or other animist, a status you obtained through lineage, apprenticeship, or a supernatural event.

feats.name: Folksy Patter
feats.description: You are adept at disguising coded messages as folksy idioms.

feats.name: Forge-Day's Rest
feats.description: Access underground dwarf ethnicity Your unusual rest cycle allows you to recover faster.

feats.name: Forlorn
feats.description: Watching your friends age and die fills you with moroseness that protects you against harmful emotions. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against emotion effects. If you roll a success on a saving throw against an emotion effect, you get a critical success instead.

feats.name: Foxfire
feats.description: A crack of your tail sparks wisps of blue energy.

feats.name: Free Heart
feats.description: You follow all your passions, unlimited in creativity and zeal.

feats.name: Friendly Nudge
feats.description: You don't always have time to wait for your allies to make room, so it's best to politely move them yourself.

feats.name: Fumesoul
feats.description: The air inside you asserts itself as a toxic miasma, inuring you to most poisons. You gain resistance to poison equal to half your level (minimum 1).

feats.name: Ganzi Lore
feats.description: Primal chaos lives in your blood and grants you unpredictable knowledge. You become trained in Deception and Performance. If you would automatically become trained in one of those skills, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You also become trained in Maelstrom Lore.

feats.name: Gemsoul
feats.description: The earth inside you asserts itself as crystallized gemstones or metals, like a cluster of quartz or veins of iron and gold.

feats.name: General Training
feats.description: Your adaptability manifests in your mastery of a range of useful abilities. You gain a 1st-level general feat. You must meet the prerequisites, but can select this only at character creation.

feats.name: Genie Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You've trained with weapons used by your genie ancestors.

feats.name: Ghoran Lore
feats.description: The very first memories ghorans have are those of their creation, and even after thousands of years, those memories have never faded for you.

feats.name: Ghoran Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: Over the course of your long, long existence, you've had a chance to practice with traditional ghoran weapons.

feats.name: Ghost Hunter
feats.description: Your connection to the Boneyard lets you harm undead as if you had a force weapon. Your unarmed attacks and weapon Strikes deal an additional 1 positive damage to undead creatures.

feats.name: Gildedsoul
feats.description: Your elemental lineage manifests in the polished gleam of precious metals, such as silver and gold, or even rare and valuable skymetals like adamantine or orichalcum.

feats.name: Gloomseer
feats.description: Gloom holds few terrors for you, and the pall of darkness over Nidal has made you comfortable in dim light. You gain low-light vision.

feats.name: Gnome Obsession
feats.description: You might have a flighty nature, but when a topic captures your attention, you dive into it headfirst.

feats.name: Gnome Polyglot
feats.description: Your extensive travels, curiosity, and love of learning help you to learn languages quickly.

feats.name: Gnome Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You favor unusual weapons tied to your people, such as blades with curved and peculiar shapes.

feats.name: Goblin Lore
feats.description: You've picked up skills and tales from your goblin community.

feats.name: Goblin Scuttle
feats.description: Trigger An ally ends a move action adjacent to you. You take advantage of your ally's movement to adjust your position. You Step.

feats.name: Goblin Song
feats.description: You sing annoying goblin songs, distracting your foes with silly and repetitive lyrics.

feats.name: Goblin Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: Others might look upon them with disdain, but you know that the weapons of your people are as effective as they are sharp.

feats.name: Goloma Courage
feats.description: The most important thing you've learned living with fear is how to overcome it.

feats.name: Goloma Lore
feats.description: You lived an insulated life focused on avoiding danger.

feats.name: Grasping Reach
feats.description: You can extend a tangle of vines or tendrils to support your arms and extend your reach.

feats.name: Gravesight
feats.description: You can see in the darkness as easily as a psychopomp. You gain darkvision. Special You can select this feat only at 1st level, and you can't retrain into or out of this feat.

feats.name: Grim Insight
feats.description: Others' attempts to scare you often grant you insights about your would-be bullies that you can then exploit.

feats.name: Grimspawn
feats.description: Your lineage traces back to a daemon, one of the manifestations of horrific forms of death that devour souls within their foul home of Abaddon.

feats.name: Grove-Harbored
feats.description: You are accustomed to exploring thick woodlands and defending yourself against the attacks of magical plants.

feats.name: Hag Claws
feats.description: You can extend your fingers into deadly, hag-like claws. You gain a claw unarmed attack that deals 1d4 slashing damage. Your claws are in the brawling group and have the agile and unarmed traits.

feats.name: Hag's Sight
feats.description: You've nurtured the supernatural abilities gifted by your hag mother, particularly your sight. You gain darkvision.

feats.name: Halfling Lore
feats.description: You've dutifully learned how to keep your balance and how to stick to the shadows where it's safe, important skills passed down through generations of halfling tradition.

feats.name: Halfling Luck
feats.description: Frequency once per day Trigger You fail a skill check or saving throw.

feats.name: Halfling Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You favor traditional halfling weapons, so you've learned how to use them more effectively.

feats.name: Halo
feats.description: You have a halo of light and goodness that sheds light with the effects of a divine @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Light] cantrip.

feats.name: Handy with your Paws
feats.description: You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to Crafting checks to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Repair] non-magical items, and you don't need a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.Repair Toolkit]{Repair Kit} for such repairs as long as you have any amount …

feats.name: Hard Tail
feats.description: Your tail is much stronger than most, and you can lash out with it with the strength of a whip. You gain a tail unarmed attack that deals 1d6 bludgeoning damage.

feats.name: Hard to Fool
feats.description: You're accustomed to seeing through shifting darkness and bewildering magic. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus on Perception checks against illusions as well as to Will saves against illusions and shadow effects.

feats.name: Harmless Doll
feats.description: You look like nothing more than an ordinary toy, doll, or statuette, and you can fool others with your innocuous appearance.

feats.name: Harmlessly Cute
feats.description: Your size and demeanor make it easy for you to convince others that you mean no harm.

feats.name: Haughty Obstinacy
feats.description: Your powerful ego makes it harder for others to order you around. If you roll a success on a saving throw against a mental effect that attempts to directly control your actions, you critically succeed instead. If a creature rolls a failure on a check to Coerce you using Intimidation, it gets a critical failure instead.

feats.name: Hellspawn
feats.description: Your lineage descends from devils, conniving schemers of Hell.

feats.name: Helpful Poppet
feats.description: You're particularly skilled at helping others with a task.

feats.name: Hidden Thorn
feats.description: Some flowers can hide their thorns, and yours happen to be hidden along your arms. You gain a thorns unarmed attack that deals 1d6 piercing damage. Your thorns are in the knife weapon group and have the finesse and unarmed traits.

feats.name: Hoarder's Craw
feats.description: Your draconic features include a pouch inside your body that you use to store food prior to digestion.

feats.name: Hobgoblin Lore
feats.description: You've studied traditional hobgoblin exercises and fieldcraft, all of which have a militaristic bent.

feats.name: Hobgoblin Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You gain access to all uncommon weapons with the hobgoblin trait.

feats.name: Hold Mark
feats.description: You bear scars or tattoos enhanced by the mark of your community's prowess.

feats.name: Howling Aspect
feats.description: Frequency once per 10 minutes Pulling your hair loose into flames and gnashing your teeth into ragged fangs, you howl a vow of ire, and your form surges to meet the demands of your deadly promise.

feats.name: Hungerseed Lore
feats.description: Your oni heritage grants you knowledge of trickery and martial traditions. You become trained in Athletics and Deception. If you would automatically become trained in one of those skills, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You also become trained in Oni Lore.

feats.name: Hungry Eyes
feats.description: Your eyes can see through darkness with an oni's visual acuity. You gain low-light vision, or you gain darkvision if you already have low-light vision. Special You can select this feat twice, once to gain low-light vision and a second time to gain darkvision.

feats.name: Hunter's Defense
feats.description: Frequency once per hour Trigger A creature with the animal, beast, elemental, fey, fungus, or plant trait attacks you, and you can see the attacker.

feats.name: Hyena Familiar
feats.description: Hyenas serve kholo as pets and trackers. Some kholos, such as yourself, draw the attention of smaller hyenas that are vessels for magical spirits. You gain a Tiny hyena as a familiar.

feats.name: Idyllkin
feats.description: Your wild, unique features betray your agathion birthright.

feats.name: Ifrit Lore
feats.description: Your fire elemental heritage connects you to forges and volcanoes. You become trained in Crafting and Nature. If you would automatically become trained in one of those skills, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You also become trained in Plane of Fire Lore.

feats.name: Illusion Sense
feats.description: Your ancestors spent their days cloaked and cradled in illusions, and as a result, sensing illusion magic is second nature to you.

feats.name: Improvisational Defender
feats.description: You are always prepared for fights that frequently disrupt seemingly peaceful times. When you use an improvised weapon or a shoddy weapon, you don't take the -2 item penalty to attack rolls.

feats.name: Inherit the Dreaming Heirloom
feats.description: Whether you always carried it or perhaps only recently inherited, you have a pusaka—an heirloom containing a spirit who you communicate with in your dreams.

feats.name: Innate Understanding
feats.description: Vague connections to the knowledge and prowess of your past help guide you in tasks that this life might not understand.

feats.name: Inner Fire (Naari)
feats.description: You can call the fire inside you into the palm of your hand. You can cast the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Ignition] cantrip as an innate primal or arcane spell at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell rank equal to half your level rounded up.

feats.name: Innocuous
feats.description: Access Jaric ethnicity Halflings have been unobtrusive assistants of larger folk for untold ages, and your people count on this assumption of innocence.

feats.name: Internal Cohesion
feats.description: Axis's order suffuses your body and helps it maintain coherency.

feats.name: Internal Compartment
feats.description: You can hide a small object of up to light Bulk inside a hollow cavity on one of your forearms.

feats.name: Intuitive Cooperation
feats.description: Access Jaric ethnicity You are accustomed to working alongside others, relying on each other to get by.

feats.name: Intuitive Crafting
feats.description: Your affinity with process and order makes you a natural artisan.

feats.name: Inventive Offensive
feats.description: You can jury-rig your weapons to perform in unexpected ways.

feats.name: Iron Belly
feats.description: A good laugh comes from the belly, and by laughing every day, yours has grown quite strong.

feats.name: Iron Fists
feats.description: Your fists have been forged by battle, your naturally tough skin and dense bone further hardened by conflict. Your fist unarmed attacks no longer have the nonlethal trait and gain the shove weapon trait.

feats.name: Irrepressible (Nephilim)
feats.description: Your slight otherworldly nature allows you to easily brush off emotional manipulation. When you roll a success on a save against an emotion effect, you get a critical success instead.

feats.name: Iruxi Armaments
feats.description: Your weapons are those you were born with.

feats.name: It Takes a Village
feats.description: Your community bond creates better dynamics when working together. You gain a +4 circumstance bonus to checks to Aid.

feats.name: Jotun's Eyes
feats.description: Your vision has adapted to the darkness between planes. You gain darkvision.

feats.name: Jotunborn Grappler
feats.description: Wrestling is a common pastime for jotunborn, and you've done your fair share.

feats.name: Jotunborn Lore
feats.description: You were taught the history of your people's planar travels as well as the skills necessary to thrive in sub-planar environments.

feats.name: Jotunborn Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You gain access to all uncommon weapons with the jotunborn trait.

feats.name: Jungle Strider
feats.description: You are adept at dodging roots, foliage, and other jungle obstacles.

feats.name: Junk Tinker
feats.description: You can make useful tools out of even twisted or rusted scraps.

feats.name: Keen Nose
feats.description: Accustomed to the foul air of underground tunnels, your sense of smell is sharpened, and noxious scents are less offensive.

feats.name: Keep up Appearances
feats.description: Access Taldan nationality Trigger You are affected by an emotion effect.

feats.name: Kholo Lore
feats.description: You paid close attention to the senior hunters in your clan to learn their tricks.

feats.name: Kholo Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You gain access to all uncommon weapons with the kholo trait.

feats.name: Kitsune Lore
feats.description: You excel at navigating social situations and keeping secrets.

feats.name: Kitsune Spell Familiarity
feats.description: You've picked up a few magical tricks. During your daily preparations, choose @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Daze], @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Forbidding Ward], or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.Ghost Sound]. Until your next daily pre…

feats.name: Know Oneself
feats.description: Frequency once per day Access Vudrani ethnicity Trigger You roll a critical failure on a saving throw against an emotion effect.

feats.name: Know Your Own
feats.description: You've spent countless hours studying the history of elves on your world and beyond and are a studied expert in your people's ways. If you critically fail a check to Recall Knowledge about elves, elven society, or elven history, you get a failure instead.

feats.name: Kobold Breath
feats.description: You channel the power of your draconic benefactor to expel a torrent of energy from your mouth that manifests as a @Template[type:line|distance:30] or a @Template[type:cone|distance:15], dealing @Damage[(ceil(@actor.level / 2))d4|options:area-damage] damage.

feats.name: Kobold Lore
feats.description: You attentively learned key kobold survival strategies from your elders.

feats.name: Kobold Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You gain access to all uncommon weapons with the kobold trait.

feats.name: Koi Secrets
feats.description: Access Tian Xia origin Due to their beautiful colors, ornamental koi are often found in parks and gardens where townsfolk roam.

feats.name: Land Legs
feats.description: You are comfortable on land for a short time. You gain a land Speed of 20 and can breathe air for 10 minutes.

feats.name: Lavasoul
feats.description: Your elemental lineage manifests as magma and molten rock, and you can focus the magma into your hand to attack your foes.

feats.name: Lawbringer
feats.description: You trace your lineage to archons, guardians of the seven-tiered mountain of Heaven and nurturers of law and virtue within mortals.

feats.name: Learn by Watching
feats.description: Before your awakening, you watched humanoids performing some skill near you frequently.

feats.name: Leech-Clip
feats.description: You are trained to capture deserters, or "leeches." Make a melee Strike with weapon from the flail group.

feats.name: Leshy Lore
feats.description: You deeply understand your people's cultural traditions and innate strengths.

feats.name: Leshy Superstition
feats.description: Trigger You attempt a saving throw against a spell or magical effect, but haven't rolled yet.

feats.name: Lesser Enhance Venom
feats.description: Your venom grows deadlier and more plentiful.

feats.name: Life-Giving Magic
feats.description: Frequency once per minute Trigger You cast an innate spell from a gnome heritage or ancestry feat.

feats.name: Lightning Tongue
feats.description: Your tongue darts out faster than the eye can see to retrieve loose objects.

feats.name: Living Weapon
feats.description: You've learned to use part of your form as a weapon.

feats.name: Lizardfolk Lore
feats.description: You listened carefully to the tales passed down among your community.

feats.name: Made for Combat
feats.description: You can use your body as a deadly weapon. You gain one of the following melee unarmed attacks, which must be one that matches your body (for instance, tsukumogami wrap for a tsukumogami that's a bolt of cotton). A blade unarmed attack that deals 1d6 slashing,…

feats.name: Maiden's Mending
feats.description: Frequency once per minute Trigger You cast a non-cantrip occult spell or an innate spell from a changeling ancestry feat.

feats.name: Mariner's Fire
feats.description: You conjure uncanny orbs of spiritual flame that float above or below the water's surface.

feats.name: Marsh Runner
feats.description: When you use the Step action, you can ignore difficult terrain caused by flooding, swamps, or quicksand.

feats.name: Mental Sustenance
feats.description: You can sustain yourself with the power of your mind. You need only a single serving of food and water each week to avoid starvation or dehydration. You can hold your breath for an additional 5 rounds before running out of air.

feats.name: Merfolk Lore
feats.description: You paid close attention to your lessons in school.

feats.name: Merfolk Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: You were taught the most effective weapons of the sea.

feats.name: Metal Skin
feats.description: Your skin has a metallic sheen and is remarkably tough. You gain resistance to slashing damage equal to half your level (minimum 1).

feats.name: Meticulous Restorer
feats.description: Waste not, want not; your steady eye allows you to restore craftwork quickly while conserving valuable material. You gain the Quick Repair feat. In addition, when you roll a critical failure while attempting to Repair an item, you get a failure instead.

feats.name: Mighty Dragonet
feats.description: You've grown especially large and now tower over your fellow dragonets. Instead of Tiny, your size is Small. Special Though you can grow larger at a higher level than 1st, you can't retrain out of this feat.

feats.name: Minotaur Lore
feats.description: You were raised in a minotaur enclave or have spent a great deal of time studying your culture and ancestry.

feats.name: Minotaur Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: Your elders taught you to wield weapons that can split skulls and smash through shields.

feats.name: Minuscule Mentee
feats.description: You've found a fellow poppet on the cusp of awakening and decided to help. You gain a poppet familiar.

feats.name: Miresoul
feats.description: You have a connection to the muddier denizens of the Plane of Earth from its border with the Plane of Water, and your elemental lineage manifests as clay or mud, reflecting that bond.

feats.name: Mirror-Risen
feats.description: You were created from magic involving mirrors and still retain some ability to seek refuge in reflected illusions. You gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Mirror-Trickery] reaction.

feats.name: Mistsoul
feats.description: You have a connection to vaporous forms of water, such as fog and mist. You gain the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Mist Blending] reaction.

feats.name: Moldersoul
feats.description: Your connection to elemental wood asserts itself in the form of decay, like forgotten logs left to rot and the char that remains after a forest fire, cycles of devastation clearing away what was to make room for the next generation of life.

feats.name: Molten Wit
feats.description: Your elemental soul has sparked not just your body but also your mind.

feats.name: Monstrous Peacemaker
feats.description: Your dual human and orc nature has given you a unique perspective, allowing you to bridge the gap between humans and the many intelligent creatures in the world that humans consider monsters.

feats.name: Moon May
feats.description: Your mother was a moon hag, a handmaiden of Groetus, and you have a single scarlet eye and a sliver of her supernal foresight.

feats.name: Morph-Risen
feats.description: You were once a shapeshifter or spellcaster using polymorph magic, but something went wrong, and you became trapped in the form of the creature you were imitating.

feats.name: Morphic Strike
feats.description: You can lash out with a portion of your previous self.

feats.name: Mountain Strategy
feats.description: Dwarves have a long history fighting common foes, and you've mastered the ancient tactics to better face these enemies.

feats.name: Musetouched
feats.description: Your blood sings with the liberating power of the azatas, living embodiments of freedom from the wild realm of Elysium.

feats.name: My Claws are Daggers
feats.description: Your claws are as sharp as your teeth. You gain a claw unarmed attack that deals 1d6 slashing damage. Your claws are in the knife group and have the agile, finesse, and unarmed traits. At 5th level, whenever you get a critical hit with your claws or jaws, you…

feats.name: Nagaji Lore
feats.description: You're the vassal or apprentice of a learned naga, and you've studied the secrets of naga magic and concocting intricate poisons.

feats.name: Nagaji Spell Familiarity
feats.description: Either through study, exposure, or familial devotion, you have the magic of nagas bubbling in your blood.

feats.name: Nalinivati's Light
feats.description: Your slit eyes expand to absorb light and can see in the darkest of places. You gain darkvision. Special You can select this feat only at 1st level, and you can't retrain into or out of this feat.

feats.name: Nanite Surge
feats.description: Frequency once per hour Trigger You attempt a skill check requiring three actions or fewer.

feats.name: Native Waters
feats.description: You were born with or obtained a special connection to either fresh water or salt water.

feats.name: Natural Ambition
feats.description: You were raised to be ambitious and always reach for the stars, leading you to progress quickly in your chosen field. You gain a 1st-level class feat for your class.

feats.name: Natural Mutagen
feats.description: You can distill your natural shapeshifting ability into an elixir that grants a lesser ability to morph.

feats.name: Natural Performer
feats.description: Entertainment comes naturally to you. You become trained in Performance and gain one 1st-level Performance skill feat.

feats.name: Natural Senses
feats.description: You have retained your sharp animal senses even after awakening.

feats.name: Natural Skill
feats.description: Your ingenuity allows you to learn a wide variety of skills. You become trained in two skills of your choice.

feats.name: Nephilim Eyes
feats.description: Your eyes can see through darkness with visual acuity akin to that possessed by most extraplanar beings. You gain darkvision.

feats.name: Nephilim Lore
feats.description: Your celestial, fiendish, or monitor heritage grants you broad planar knowledge. You become trained in Diplomacy and Religion. If you would automatically become trained in one of those skills, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You also become trained in a Lore skill related to your planar lineage.

feats.name: Nightvision Adaptation
feats.description: The nanites in your ocular processors have adapted to darkness, enhancing your ability to see in the dark. You gain darkvision.

feats.name: Nimble Elf
feats.description: Your muscles are tightly honed. Your speed increases by 5 feet.

feats.name: Nimble Hooves
feats.description: You possess hooves, ankle wings, or some other feature that hastens your movement. Your Speed increases by 5 feet. Special The Speed increase from this feat isn't cumulative with any Speed increase from other ancestry feats.

feats.name: Nocturnal Charm
feats.description: The supernatural charm of your vampiric progenitor hangs about you, and you've learned to use it on mortals.

feats.name: Nocturnal Tripkee
feats.description: You tend to do most of your hunting and work at night and have adapted to the requirements of nocturnal life.

feats.name: Nosoi's Mask
feats.description: Many psychopomps wear masks when dealing with the living, and many cultures believe that seeing an unmasked psychopomp's face invites death.

feats.name: Occult Dragonblood
feats.description: Your blood contains a tiny fragment of unusual or inexplicable power from a mysterious dragon, such as a conspirator dragon or omen dragon.

feats.name: Ocean Wariness
feats.description: Stories about those who have mistreated your people in the past (particularly stories about alghollthu mind control) left you untrusting of those outside your community.

feats.name: Ocean's Bite
feats.description: Your jaws open wider than a humanoid mouth should, showing off multiple rows of serrated shark teeth or a viperfish-like nest of needles. You gain a jaws unarmed attack that deals 1d8 piercing damage. Your jaws are in the brawling group.

feats.name: Old Soul
feats.description: Your immortal lineage has granted you a lifespan much longer than most mortals, and you've learned a lot along the way. You become trained in two skills of your choice.

feats.name: One-Toed Hop
feats.description: Assuming a peculiar stance, you make a short hop on each toe. You make a vertical @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Leap] without triggering reactions that can be triggered by move actions or upon leaving or entering a square.

feats.name: Oni Form
feats.description: Your horns flash briefly as you grow in size and ferocity.

feats.name: Oni Sight
feats.description: Your oni heritage grants you supernatural sight. You gain darkvision.

feats.name: Oni Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: Oni prefer large, cruel weapons for smashing their foes to pieces, and so do you.

feats.name: Open Mind
feats.description: You have inherent psychic abilities that allow you to tap into the power of your mind.

feats.name: Orc Ferocity
feats.description: Fierceness in battle runs through your blood, and you refuse to fall from your injuries. When you would be reduced to 0 Hit Points, you can use your reaction to remain at 1 Hit Point instead. You gain the wounded 1 condition (or increase your wounded condition by 1). You can use this ability once per day.

feats.name: Orc Lore
feats.description: The hold elders taught you your people's histories, told tales of great athletic feats, and shared with you the hardships your ancestors endured so that you can pass this wisdom down to future generations.

feats.name: Orc Sight
feats.description: Your orc blood grants you the keen vision of your forebears.

feats.name: Orc Superstition
feats.description: Trigger You attempt a saving throw against a spell or magical effect, and have not yet rolled.

feats.name: Orc Warmask
feats.description: You paint your face to create a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.equipment-srd.Item.Orc Warmask]{Warmask} , an invested magic item of negligible Bulk with an item level equal to your level and a usage of worn (mask).

feats.name: Orc Weapon Familiarity
feats.description: In combat, you favor the brutal weapons that are traditional for your orc ancestors.

feats.name: Oread Lore
feats.description: Your earth elemental heritage grounds your understanding of the physical world. You become trained in Athletics and Nature. If you would automatically become trained in one of those skills, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You also become trained in Plane of Earth Lore.

feats.name: Otherworldly Magic
feats.description: Your elven magic manifests as a simple arcane spell, even if you aren't formally trained in magic. Choose one cantrip from the arcane spell list. You can cast this cantrip as an arcane innate spell at will. A cantrip is heightened to a spell rank equal to half your level rounded up.

feats.name: Overlooked Mastermind
feats.description: Many consider half-orcs little more than dumb brutes.

feats.name: Pack Hunter
feats.description: You were taught how to hunt as part of a pack. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to checks to Aid, and your allies gain a +2 circumstance bonus to checks to Aid you.

feats.name: Pack Rat
feats.description: You've learned how to cram vast quantities into small spaces.

feats.name: Pantheon Magic
feats.description: The touch of divine meddling still runs through you, whether you want it or not.

feats.name: Parthenogenic Hatchling
feats.description: You were hatched from an unfertilized egg during hard times for your people, and are a biological copy of your mother.

feats.name: Pelagic Aptitude
feats.description: You've learned special tricks to survive underwater.

feats.name: Perfect Dive
feats.description: You dive into the water with exceptional skill and connection to the waves, urging the water itself to cushion your fall. You intentionally Leap or otherwise fall into the water, taking no falling damage regardless of the distance.

feats.name: Phantom Visage
feats.description: You cloak yourself in illusions to mask your nature.

feats.name: Pheromonal Message
feats.description: Your communicative pheromones are particularly well developed from years of living with other athamarus.

feats.name: Pierce the Darkness
feats.description: Your eyes have adapted to see in all circumstances, even without the assistance of light.

feats.name: Pitborn
feats.description: Your blood bears the mark of a demon, a living embodiment of sin from the fetid depths of the Outer Rifts.

feats.name: Plane-Stepping Dash
feats.description: Frequency once per day You move with magical swiftness, treading between planar boundaries to shorten your journey.

feats.name: Play Dead
feats.description: You lie @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone] and pretend to be an ordinary skeleton.

feats.name: Plumekith
feats.description: Your blood soars with the power of the magnificent garuda, proud and stoic avian warriors. You only take half the normal damage from falling and gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saving throws against effects with the air trait.

feats.name: Political Acumen
feats.description: You have spent years following the political maneuvering and secret alliances of elves in Iadara.

feats.name: Polymorphic Escape
feats.description: Trigger You would become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Grabbed], @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Immobilized], @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Prone], or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Restrained].

feats.name: Practiced Brawn
feats.description: You're accustomed to long days filled with hard physical labor.

feats.name: Prairie Rider
feats.description: You grew up riding your clan's shaggy ponies and riding dogs.

feats.name: Preordained Path
feats.description: The orderly nature of the cosmos grants you foresight. Once per day, you can reroll a failed skill check and take the second result.

feats.name: Primal Dragonblood
feats.description: A dragon with a deep connection to the natural world, such as an adamantine dragon or a horned dragon, resides somewhere on your family tree.

feats.name: Progenitor Lore
feats.description: You didn't just inherit your physical form from your progenitor; you also retained some of their knowledge.

feats.name: Proteankin
feats.description: Your blood bubbles with the roiling quintessence of the Maelstrom, the infinite sea of primal chaos that long ago spawned the other Outer Planes.

feats.name: Proximity Alert
feats.description: You're unnaturally in tune with your surroundings and react instinctively to danger. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks made as initiative rolls.

feats.name: Puncturing Horn
feats.description: While many kashrishi never use their horns for more than cracking open hard-shelled fruits or amplifying their psychic powers, you've practiced using yours offensively.

feats.name: Pyrophilic Recovery
feats.description: You rely on fire and extreme heat to sprout and regrow.

feats.name: Quadruped
feats.description: You were crafted in a form with four legs rather than two. Your Speed is 30 feet. Special You can take this feat only at 1st level, and you can't retrain into or out of this feat.

feats.name: Quah Bond
feats.description: Access Shoanti ethnicity You grew up among the Shoanti tribes, with the spirits watching over you, and they offer you guidance.

feats.name: Quick Shape
feats.description: Trigger You roll initiative. Your instincts kick in and you take on an aggressive stance. You use @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Change Shape] to enter your hybrid form or a new form granted by a beastkin feat.

feats.name: Quicksoul
feats.description: The elemental metal in your bloodline literally courses through your veins in the form of liquid metals like mercury, rubidium, gallium, and djezet, giving your actions a languid fluidity.

feats.name: Radiant Circuitry
feats.description: Your biological circuitry emits light like a torch, casting bright light in a 20-foot radius (and dim light for the next 20 feet). The light shuts off when you take this action again or are knocked @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Unconscious].

feats.name: Rat Familiar
feats.description: You have a pet rat that has become magically bonded to you. You gain a familiar, and this familiar must be a rat. It still gets the benefits of familiar abilities, but its base form remains a rat.

feats.name: Ratfolk Lore
feats.description: Years of experience among ratfolk communities have made you nimble, and you've learned to run and hide when enemies threaten.

feats.name: Ratspeak
feats.description: You can ask questions of, receive answers from, and use the Diplomacy skill with rodents, including beavers, mice, porcupines, rats, and squirrels, but not with other mammals, such as dogs or bats. The GM determines which animals count as rodents.

feats.name: Razzle-Dazzle
feats.description: Frequency once per hour Trigger You blind or dazzle a creature.

feats.name: Reassuring Presence
feats.description: Trigger An ally within 30 feet gains the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Frightened] condition.

feats.name: Refined Motion in Darkness
feats.description: The ability to move elegantly and undetected through darkness aided your people as they fled the Netherworld.

feats.name: Reflection Lore
feats.description: Your existence as a duplicate gives you unique insights. You become trained in Deception and Occultism. If you would automatically become trained in one of those skills, you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You also become trained in Reflection Lore.

feats.name: Reflective Defense
feats.description: Frequency once per 10 minutes Trigger A creature within 30 feet of you targets you, and you can see the attacker.

feats.name: Reinforced Chassis
feats.description: Your body is designed to be particularly resilient.

feats.name: Remnants of the Past
feats.description: Memories of your first life are strong and continually return to you, even after several incarnations.

feats.name: Remorseless Lash
feats.description: You're skilled at beating an enemy when their morale is already breaking.

feats.name: Reptile Rider
feats.description: To you, crocodilians, giant lizards, and dinosaurs are loyal steeds, not monsters.

feats.name: Reptile Speaker
feats.description: You hear the sounds of reptiles as language. You can ask questions of, receive answers from, and use the Diplomacy skill with animals that are reptiles (the GM determines which animals count as reptiles).

feats.name: Retractable Claws
feats.description: You gain a claw unarmed attack that deals 1d4 slashing damage. Your claws are in the brawling group and have the agile, finesse, and unarmed traits.

feats.name: Riftmarked
feats.description: Long ago, qlippoth marked someone in your lineage, most likely through their ancient runestones that dot Golarion's surface.

feats.name: Rimesoul
feats.description: The water inside you is cold and frozen, like sheets of ice and frigid glaciers.

feats.name: Rivethun Disciple
feats.description: Access Rivethun Adherent background or you are a member of the Rivethun By studying with the dwarven shamans of Highhelm, you have become aware of the friction between your body and spirit.

feats.name: Rock Runner
feats.description: Your innate connection to stone makes you adept at moving across uneven surfaces.

feats.name: Rough Rider
feats.description: You are especially good at riding traditional goblin mounts.

feats.name: Round Ears
feats.description: Your ears are a blend of elven and human. You can pass as a member of either your ancestry or elf without needing a Deception check. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Deception checks to Impersonate a member of those ancestries.

feats.name: Ru-shi
feats.description: You were born to a jiang-shi, one of the so-called hopping vampires found most often in Tian-Xia.

feats.name: Saber Teeth
feats.description: You have long fangs, natural or augmented. You gain a jaws unarmed attack that deals 1d6 piercing damage. Your jaws are in the brawling group and have the unarmed trait.

feats.name: Sage of Scattered Leaves
feats.description: The epics of your people's tragic yet proud history blaze like burning leaves in the forests of ancestral memory.

feats.name: Samsaran Lore
feats.description: You have untold ages of knowledge beneath your belt, focused on the pursuit of enlightenment.

feats.name: Samsaran Weapon Memory
feats.description: The training of your past lives and even of those of samsarans who came before is deep-seated and guides your movements in combat.

feats.name: Saoc Astrology
feats.description: Frequency 3 times per day Access Lirgeni nationality Requirements You must spend 10 minutes just after your daily preparations examining the sky or consulting a star chart or you can't use this action that day.

feats.name: Sarangay Lore
feats.description: You listened carefully to the tales passed down among your community.

feats.name: Scales of Steel
feats.description: Your scales are hard as armor, ensuring you always have some protection in the dangerous world.

feats.name: Scaly Hide
feats.description: You were born with a layer of scales across your entire body that resemble those of your draconic progenitor.

feats.name: Scamper
feats.description: Requirements You are adjacent to at least one enemy.

feats.name: Scamper Underfoot
feats.description: You are used to ducking under foes and can scurry around the battlefield with ease.

feats.name: Scavenger's Search
feats.description: You're always on the lookout for supplies and valuables. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to locate objects (including secret doors and hazards) you search for within 30 feet with a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.Seek] action.

feats.name: Scholar's Inheritance
feats.description: Your parents devoted themselves to alchemical experiments involving elemental energy.

feats.name: Scorched on the Crackling Mountain
feats.description: By ritualistically marking your fur with fire, like an infamous tanuki of legend, you protect yourself against future flames.

feats.name: Scuttle Up
feats.description: Your environment requires you to climb cave walls or tree trunks with great regularity. You gain a Climb speed of 10 feet.

feats.name: Sea Legs
feats.description: You are comfortable on the water for short bursts. You gain a swim Speed of 10 feet and can breathe underwater for 5 minutes.
prerequisites: You don't have a swim Speed.

feats.name: Seasong
feats.description: Sound carries far beneath the waves, and your people have strong, enchanting voices.

feats.name: Seedpod
feats.description: Your body produces a nearly endless supply of hard seedpods.

feats.name: Sensitive Nose
feats.description: Your large black nose isn't just for show.

feats.name: Sequestered Spell
feats.description: You consumed so much of a type of magic as a larva that you can now cast it purely on instinct.

feats.name: Serpent's Tongue
feats.description: You subconsciously flick your tongue through the air to taste the world around you. You gain imprecise scent with a range of 30 feet.

feats.name: Seven Changes Performance
feats.description: While kitsune are known for their magical illusion powers, you're skilled at stage magic, especially costuming and quick changes.

feats.name: Shackleborn
feats.description: Your lineage shows the signs of velstrac tampering, including some curious quirk bequeathed by those shadowy surgeons of flesh and souls.

feats.name: Shadow Blending
feats.description: Trigger A creature attempts a flat check to target you while you're @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Concealed] or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.Hidden] due to dim light or darkness.

feats.name: Shadow of the Wilds
feats.description: It's difficult to notice your passage through wild areas.

feats.name: Shapechanger's Intuition
